title: ‘Have done a pious job’: Follower-turned-whistleblower of arrested Nashik godman reveals shocking details
content: Ashok Kharat is currently lodged in police custody in a rape and black magic case; allegations against him in the high-profile case has caused ripples in Maharashtra politics

title: NASA’s Artemis II mission will be a grand success: ISRO chairman V. Narayanan
content: Recalling past human landings on the moon, Dr. Narayanan said the Artemis programme is a step towards repeating the feat

title: HC order triggered by government inaction on fee reimbursement transfers burden to students
content: While college managements termed the HC order as a historic move the students’ union, Opposition party leaders and the students want the government to release the dues and resolve the issue

title: Hazardous chemicals found in milk during raids across East Godavari
content: Animal Husbandry and Food Safety authorities intensify inspections after 16 deaths linked to ethylene glycol poisoning in Rajamahendravaram; 15 of 705 samples test positive for urea, detergents and maltodextrin

title: West Asia conflict hits coffee prices; Karnataka growers hold back produce
content: Planters say prices, which remained stable and favourable during the harvesting season from December 2025 to February 2026, began to fall soon after the conflict escalated in March.

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: PM Modi holds roadshow in Kerala; Rahul Gandhi reiterates BJP-LDF nexus to defeat UDF
content: The real culprits behind Malda violence escaped and the NIA targets innocent people, says West Bengal CM Mamata

title: IIITDM Kurnool bags fourth spot in Inter-IIIT Sports Meet
content: IIITM Gwalior emerges first in the overall institute rankings, followed by IIIT Dharwad, IIITDM Kancheepuram

title: War of words intensifies in poll-bound Assam
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says top Congress leaders will have to be reborn to put him behind bars

title: High school teachers and PU lecturers say their academic calendar clashes with census work
content: Teachers and lecturers have urged the govt. to release them from Census 2027 work, as they argue that it is difficult to evaluate answer sheets, work as enumerators and conduct online special classes simultaneously

title: Miniature artist from Yemmiganur carves a niche for himself
content: Having made it to India Book of Records and the Guinness Book, Shiva Keshava Machani has been conferred with Ugadi Puraskarams for his contribution to the art form over the past 12 years

title: BRS condemns ‘attack’ on KCR’s camp office at Gajwel, demands arrest of those involved
content: Party leaders ask ruling Congress whether attacking elected  representatives of opposition parties is “Indiramma Rajyam”

title: Locals attack police, snatch their arms after a man dies in custody in Bihar’s Katihar
content: Police claim that the youth tried to end his life by hanging himself in the cell but died on the way to the hospital; victim’s family claims he was arrested a day earlier than claimed and tortured

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026: Tamil-speaking electorate holds key to Devikulam constituency
content: The Devikulam constituency will feature a notable “battle of Rajas,” with candidates F. Raja from the UDF, A. Raja from the LDF, and S. Rajendran of the NDA. Rajendran’s shift to the BJP could disrupt traditional voting patterns, while local residents express concerns over unresolved issues like housing and healthcare.

title: Close contest between three-time MLA and new face with family legacy
content: Among the key issues before the people is the long-pending demand of increased compensation for those who lost land to the Upper Krishna Project; another issue is the plan to expand the irrigated area for the implementation of Phase 3 of the project

title: Pinarayi slams Rahul for drawing equivalence between LDF and Hindutva forces in Chhattisgarh
content: Kerala Chief Minister says the Congress leader’s “brazenly misleading” statement smacks of an “impudence stemming from social and political illiteracy and ignorance”; he says the Congress’ “complacency and ideological ambiguity” in politically resisting Hindu majoritarian forces, including their trespasses on minorities, paved the way for the ascendancy of the Sangh Parivar-backed BJP in India

title: Jagan’s ‘MAVIGUN’ plan a new political drama, says Agriculture Minister Atchannaidu
content: The former Chief Minister destroyed the Amaravati project when in power, he says, and asserts that the NDA government brought the capital works back on track to drive growth across all regions

title: 'All they care about is politics and power': PM Modi slams Cong for 'endangering' Indians in Gulf
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused Congress leaders of endangering Indians in the Middle East with their remarks amid the US-Iran conflict. He stated that Congress's statements risk angering Gulf countries, potentially jeopardizing the safety of lakhs of Keralites working there. Modi emphasized his commitment to their security, highlighting India's strong relations with Gulf governments who consider Indians as family.

title: 'BJP to take away Lakshmi Bhandar money': CM Mamata to woman voters in Bengal
content: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee has warned women voters against sharing bank details with BJP workers, alleging a plot to commit financial fraud and steal benefits like Lakshmi Bhandar. She also criticized the Congress for a lack of coordination on electoral issues and failing to stand with the people during difficult times.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
content: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: Who is Suresh? Meet 21-yo Indian boxer who knocked out World No. 1
content: Indian boxer Vishvanath Suresh shocked world No. 1 Sanzhar Tashkenbay with a knockout at the Asian Championships, reaching the semifinals. The Chennai-born national champion has risen quickly through strong youth and senior performances. With multiple medals and sharp skills, he has emerged as a promising contender, signaling a bright future for Indian boxing on the global stage.

title: Reason the 6am email from 'Oracle leadership' told thousands of laid-off employees
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees receiving termination emails from "Oracle Leadership" at 6AM saying their roles had been eliminated after "careful consideration of Oracle's current business needs." Around 10,000 employees have reportedly been cut so far, with estimates suggesting the total could reach 30,000. The email gave employees a narrow window to act before systems were deactivated.

title: Sooryavanshi, Jaiswal script IPL history, become fastest Indian pair to...
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the fastest Indian pair to 500 IPL partnership runs, reaching the mark in just 248 balls and breaking Sehwag-Gambhir’s record. Rajasthan Royals, batting first after winning the toss, looked strong. Gujarat Titans, led by Rashid Khan in Shubman Gill’s absence, faced added pressure against the in-form opening duo.

title: Another global trade chokepoint at risk? After Hormuz, Iran signals Bab el-Mandeb move
content: Iran's top parliamentarian has hinted at extending maritime pressure to the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a vital global trade route, amid ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This strategic move could significantly impact global energy and supply chains, affecting countries like India that rely heavily on these waterways for trade and energy imports.

title: OpenClaw creator, who Sam Altman hired for millions, reacts to Anthropic banning his AI Agent
content: Anthropic has stopped supporting OpenClaw for its Claude subscriptions. This decision follows a recent leak of Anthropic's Claude Code source code. Anthropic cites increased demand and usage patterns of third-party tools as reasons for the change. Subscribers will receive credits and can purchase discounted usage bundles. OpenClaw's creator expressed disappointment but acknowledged efforts to mitigate the impact.

title: IPL: Rizvi's masterclass floors Mumbai; Delhi make it two in a row
content: Sameer Rizvi’s explosive 90 powered Delhi Capitals to a five-wicket win over Mumbai Indians, sealing their second straight victory. Despite early struggles, 22-year-old Rizvi. who came out to bat as Impact Sub dominated the chase, overshadowing Suryakumar Yadav’s fifty and guiding Delhi home comfortably.

title: Rajinikanth on Jailer 2, KHxRK while dodging Tamil Nadu poll questions
content: Superstar Rajinikanth has confirmed 'Jailer 2' is nearing completion and will soon enter its final production stages, with the release date to be announced by the production team. He also revealed that a new project, tentatively titled 'KHxRK,' with director Nelson Dilipkumar is slated to commence shooting in August, keeping the actor's schedule packed.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 17: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1,000 crore India net
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is on the verge of history, eyeing the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone on day 17. Starring Ranveer Singh, the spy thriller has amassed Rs. 963.03 crore domestically and Rs. 1,523.58 crore globally. Despite ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film continues to shatter records, surpassing SS Rajamouli's 'Baahubali 2' in North America and outperforming the Shah Rukh Khan starrer 'Jawan' and Yash starrer 'KGF 2' in its trajectory.

title: 'Felt like falling out of sky': Artemis II astronaut on Moon-bound journey
content: Artemis II astronauts have surpassed 150,000 miles from Earth, nearing the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen described the translunar injection burn as feeling like 'falling out of the sky.' This historic mission marks a significant step towards future lunar exploration.

title: 'Ghayal hoon isliye ghatak hoon': Raghav Chadha tears into AAP charges with Dhurandhar reference
content: Raghav Chadha refuted allegations after being replaced as AAP's Rajya Sabha deputy leader, calling it a "coordinated attack." He denied claims of not joining opposition walkouts, refusing to sign an impeachment motion, and raising "trivial issues." Chadha asserted he has consistently raised significant public concerns in Parliament over his four-year tenure.

title: What would aliens make of us? Why UFO fascination is rising again in America
content: As America eyes the moon again, UFO interest surges, fueled by Obama's 'real' alien comment and Trump's promise of file releases. Scientists ponder alien judgment of human conflict, while pop culture reflects our own fears. The Pentagon acknowledges UAPs but finds no extraterrestrial link, yet some claim 'nonhuman intelligence' is real, urging transparency.

title: BCB meets in Dhaka, talks India ties amid push for better BCCI relations
content: The meeting took place after​​ a communication had earlier been sent to the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) regarding upcoming bilateral tours and cooperation.

title: Watch: Magnus Carlsen poses for selfie, then gets her phone seized
content: At the Grenke Chess Festival, Alua Nurman took a selfie with Magnus Carlsen before their match. Though he agreed, Carlsen reported it, leading officials to confiscate her phone under strict anti-cheating rules. The game continued normally, with Carlsen winning. A similar incident happened last year, highlighting how strictly chess authorities enforce rules, even in harmless situations like selfies.

title: 11-year-old Indian-origin Bodhana becomes England No. 1
content: Indian-origin chess talent Bodhana Sivanandan, 11, is now England’s top-rated female chess player with a 2366 FIDE rating, surpassing Lan Yao. She began during lockdown, now ranks 72nd globally, and is a Woman International Master. She beat Mariya Muzychuk and earned praise from former UK Prime Minister and chess officials.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: The big Annamalai question — what BJP gains, what it risks
content: BJP leader K Annamalai clarified he chose not to contest the Tamil Nadu assembly elections to focus on campaigning for the party and allies across multiple states. His decision, accepted by the leadership, allows him a broader organizational role, aiming to boost NDA candidates' prospects.

title: ‘Devil’ and gang: UP ATS cracks Pakistan-backed module targeting rail infrastructure
content: Uttar Pradesh ATS arrested four individuals for allegedly operating a terror module directed by Pakistani handlers. The group planned arson attacks on railway infrastructure and conducted reconnaissance of key installations across multiple cities, aiming to create panic and economic disruption. They were apprehended before executing a major attack near Lucknow railway station.

title: Why did Suryakumar Yadav replace Hardik Pandya as MI captain?
content: Suryakumar Yadav led Mumbai Indians against Delhi Capitals.  Hardik Pandya was unwell and missed the match.  Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel won the toss and chose to bowl.  Mumbai Indians made several changes to their playing eleven.  The match took place at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi.  Both teams announced their playing XIs and impact substitutes.

title: Trump mulls big Cabinet shake-up as Iran war backlash deepens: Report
content: US President Trump is reportedly considering a significant Cabinet reshuffle amid growing concerns over the Iran war's political fallout and rising fuel prices.  Frustration over falling approval ratings and midterm election anxieties are fueling discussions about replacing senior officials, including intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard and commerce secretary Howard Lutnick.  The administration faces pressure to project strength and address economic worries.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo not selfish: Jorge Jesus explains Al-Nassr sub
content: Cristiano Ronaldo made a strong return for Al-Nassr in their 5-2 win over Al-Najma in the Saudi Pro League on April 3. The Portuguese star had been out for nearly a month due to a hamstring injury. Despite the break, he looked sharp on the field. He also looked confident on his comeback. Ronaldo scored twice in the second half. The icon's goals helped his team secure a comfortable victory. His performance reminded fans of his importance to the squad.

title: A land that floats, a deer that ‘dances’: Inside world’s only floating national park in Manipur
content: Manipur's Keibul Lamjao National Park, home to the world's only floating national park and the endangered Sangai deer, faces significant challenges. Rediscovered from near extinction, the Sangai's survival is intrinsically linked to the delicate phumdi ecosystem, now threatened by climate change and habitat degradation.

title: Delhi govt tightens noose: Businesses to get LPG only if they apply for PNG connection
content: Delhi's commercial LPG supply now requires users to register for piped natural gas (PNG) where available, or declare intent to switch. Oil marketing companies will verify these applications, sharing data with IGL. Businesses needing both can seek exemptions, ensuring compliance with the government's push for PNG adoption.

title: Why is Hardik Pandya not playing vs Delhi Capitals?
content: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel won the toss and chose to bowl against Mumbai Indians.  Mumbai Indians faced a setback as captain Hardik Pandya was absent due to illness, with Suryakumar Yadav leading the team.  Changes were made to Mumbai's lineup, including the inclusion of Deepak Chahar and Bosch.

title: Why Pat Cummins left SRH camp and travelled back home - Explained
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad's Pat Cummins has flown back to Australia for a scheduled scan on his injured back, a condition that previously ruled him out of the T20 World Cup.  The pacer had a brief stint with the SRH camp for light training. Cricket Australia will oversee the assessment, with Ishan Kishan continuing as captain in Cummins' absence.

title: Watch: 60-foot-high swing ride carrying 80 people collapses in UP
content: A 60-foot-high swing ride at the Bhainsaha Mela in Kushinagar collapsed mid-operation, injuring at least 30 people, including women and children. The overcrowded ride reportedly failed due to a weak, improperly anchored base. Rescue efforts were challenging, with critically injured individuals transferred to hospitals.

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with drones: 5 killed, 19 injured as strikes hit Nikopol market
content: A Russian drone strike on a market in Nikopol, Ukraine, resulted in at least five deaths and 19 injuries, including a critically wounded 14-year-old girl. The prosecutor general's office condemned the attack as a war crime. This incident follows a pattern of increased Russian strikes on Ukraine, with other attacks also causing casualties.

title: India rejects 'payment issues' claim over Iranian crude tanker's diversion to China
content: Reports of an Iranian crude oil shipment rerouted to China due to payment issues have been officially denied by India's Petroleum Ministry. The ministry clarified that such diversions are common in global oil trade for optimization and assured that Indian refiners have secured their energy supplies, including from Iran, with no payment hurdles.

title: Temple today, mosque tomorrow: It’s election season
content: As elections approach, political campaigns are transcending religious and caste boundaries. Leaders from various parties are seen participating in religious ceremonies across different faiths and visiting community memorials. This strategic shift aims to consolidate a significant minority vote and appeal to all segments of the electorate.

title: ‘This has happened before’: How science explains Déjà Vu and the feeling of life on replay
content: An uncanny feeling of having lived a moment before, known as déjà vu, is explored by scientists. This sensation, often dismissed as a trick of the mind, is now understood as a healthy brain's 'fact-checking' mechanism detecting a mismatch between familiarity and reality. Researchers suggest it reveals much about memory and perception.

title: Salman Khan’s Panvel farmhouse: Gym to stables, pool and more
content: Salman Khan's luxurious Panvel estate, Arpita Farms, sprawls over 150 acres and carries a valuation of Rs 80 crores, presenting an opulent haven. This picturesque getaway boasts earthy decor, a breathtaking pool area highlighted by a serene Buddha statue, and a full-fledged gym ideal for fitness enthusiasts.

title: 'Nothing left in Left': Rahul Gandhi alleges ‘BJP-RSS-LDF nexus’ during Kerala poll rally
content: Rahul Gandhi accused Kerala's LDF and BJP of a hidden understanding, claiming Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan aligns with those attacking minorities nationwide. Gandhi highlighted a senior Left leader's presence as proof of a fundamental shift in the LDF, asserting it has lost its 'Left' identity and is now guided by a communal 'hidden hand' in partnership with the BJP-RSS..

title: India’s Bangladesh tour back on track? BCB reaches out to BCCI for September series
content: The Bangladesh Cricket Board has reached out to the BCCI to explore new avenues of cricketing cooperation, aiming to strengthen bilateral ties. This comes after a period of strained relations, but recent diplomatic improvements suggest a positive outlook for future engagements between the two nations.

title: '17 calls between Eknath Shinde & Nashik astrologer Ashok Kharat': Activist Anjali Damania
content: Maharashtra Industries Minister Uday Samant has demanded an investigation into the leak of sensitive information concerning astrologer Ashok Kharat's rape case. This follows activist Anjali Damania's claims of high-profile politicians, including Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde, contacting the accused. Samant dismissed the allegations as a smear campaign against Shinde.

title: 'Please don’t tell my mother': 23-year-old lands in ICU after selling his kidney
content: A young man, Ayush, allegedly sold his kidney to fund his MBA education due to severe financial hardship. He received only a portion of the promised payment and is now in the ICU battling for his life. The incident highlights the desperation faced by vulnerable individuals in pursuit of education.

title: Learn to face challenges and failures with grit, Lokesh tells students
content: Citing his own example of how hard work leads to success, the HRD Minister says that the word ‘FAIL’ means ‘First Attempt In Learning’

title: Forest official clears proposal for diversion of over 10 acres of forest land for Sigandoor temple activities in Shivamogga
content: The trust that manages Sigandoor temple had sought the forest land for its activities; interestingly, the structures it proposed to develop in the said land already exist

title: Telugu inscription of Achyuta Deva Raya time found in Sri Chintala Venkataramana Swamy temple at Tadipatri: Historian MyNaa Swamy
content: MyNaa Swamy appealed to historians and research scholars to focus on the architectural wealth and history of these temples and make them more visible to the outside world

title: Kerala’s wait for high-speed rail to linger on
content: The Union government had kept the DPR of the semi-high-speed rail project submitted by Kerala under the wraps for years

title: YSRCP leader Vanitha alleges deterioration in women’s safety in A.P.
content: Police are failing to register complaints made by the victims and targeting the leaders of the opposition parties, says the former Home Minister

title: How UConn-Illinois, Arizona-Michigan will be decided in men's Final Four
content: The road ends in Indianapolis. ESPN's college basketball experts break down keys to the national semifinals.

title: 'Frustrated' Geno, Staley have fiery sideline dustup
content: UConn's Geno Auriemma ripped into the officiating crew during a live TV interview and then got into a heated argument with Dawn Staley in the final seconds of the Huskies' 62-48 loss to South Carolina in the women's Final Four.

title: UCLA grinds out sloppy win, reaches 1st title game
content: It wasn't pretty, but UCLA survived 23 turnovers and held off a late surge from Texas to pull out a 51-44 win and reach the NCAA title game for the first time in program history.

title: Mavs' Flagg, 19, is youngest with 50-point game
content: Mavericks forward Cooper Flagg became the youngest player in NBA history to have a 50-point game, as the rookie scored 51 in a 138-127 home loss to the Magic.

title: Sources: NBA investigating Bucks, Giannis' health
content: The NBA is investigating the Bucks' handling of Giannis Antetokounmpo, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania, after the star forward told reporters that he is being held out despite being healthy enough to play.

title: Haaland hat trick as Man City thrashes Liverpool
content: Manchester City beat Liverpool 4-0 in the FA Cup quarterfinals on Saturday, with Erling Haaland scoring a hat trick.

title: Doncic set to miss at least rest of regular season
content: Luka Doncic has a Grade 2 hamstring strain and will be out at least through the end of the regular season, the Lakers announced.

title: Coaches push back on LeBron's Memphis remark
content: A pair of NBA head coaches defended Memphis and its culture following criticism by Lakers star LeBron James that the Grizzlies should relocate.

title: 'Difference-maker': Griffin dazzles in Pirates debut
content: Pittsburgh Pirates shortstop Konnor Griffin made his major league debut and hit an RBI double as Pittsburgh beat the Baltimore Orioles 5-4 in its home opener at PNC Park.

title: 💰 How to bet the men's basketball semifinals
content: Will the Illini get revenge for the loss to UConn back in November? Is the total too high in the late game?

title: Latest NBA buzz: What we know about Luka's hamstring injury
content: Here's what league insiders are talking about behind the scenes, including when Doncic might be able to return from his latest injury.

title: Welcome to Mullins Drive: How a small Indiana town is embracing its new March legend
content: The UConn freshman inked his name in Indiana basketball history. His hometown added it to street signs, too.

title: NHL playoff watch: Guide to all 15 games on Statement Saturday
content: More playoff spots (and eliminations) can be clinched around the league. Here's what to watch in each contest.

title: Who will be the best RBs in the NFL draft class? We gave projections and comps for top prospects
content: We have stat projections for the top running back prospects in the 2026 NFL draft class, from Jeremiyah Love to late-round sleepers.

title: End of the feature back? What the rise of running back tandems means for the NFL draft
content: Splitting time used to be a mark against rushers. With top NFL draft prospects coming from the same backfield, that might be changing.

title: Three big trends from Portland, Toronto picks
content: The biggest (and, in some cases, surprising) picks for the Fire and Tempo had a few things in common.

title: Haaland hits long-awaited Man City hat trick as pressure piles on Liverpool's Slot
content: Erling Haaland's first Manchester City hat trick since August 2024 helped send his side to the FA Cup semifinals in a 4-0 thrashing over Liverpool.

title: The incredibly rare thing you probably missed during the Cubs' 2016 curse-breaking World Series win
content: While the Cubs were making history in Cleveland 10 years ago, one unique accomplishment flew well under the radar.

title: Final NBA MVP (and ROY) straw poll: Here's who voters are picking to win
content: Can Wemby's late push unseat SGA? Tim Bontemps polls 100 NBA insiders for the latest MVP snapshot. Plus, a look at the race for top rookie.

title: UConn on brink of dual title appearance brings 2014 to mind
content: UConn men's and women's basketball are both in the Final Four. Could the school make its first dual national championship appearance since 2014? The world was quite different back then.

title: Who are the 39 best men's U21 players in the world?
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United, Man City pushing for Anderson
content: Manchester United and Manchester City are moving quickly to sign Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Alvarez a top-10 bat? Ohtani wins Cy Young? Perdomo for real? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Keepers, sleepers and more: Top 10 notes for next season
content: As the NHL season nears its end, here are the keepers, sleepers and bouncebacks to know for next season.

title: 2 Drones, 1 Minute Apart: How Iran Hit CIA Station Inside US Embassy In Saudi
content: The US Embassy in Riyadh, like most American diplomatic posts around the world, does not operate its own air defence systems.

title: "Your Sons More In Danger With Trump": Iran To US Woman Claiming Pilot Son Missing
content: Iran downed two US military aircraft in separate incidents on Friday. One crew member was rescued, while at least one remains missing.

title: 'Politics Will Come And Go, My Priority Is Safety Of Keralites': PM Modi
content: Speaking at a BJP-led NDA rally in Pathanamthitta district, PM Modi said that the saffron party workers in the state have signalled a strong mood in Kerala to bid farewell to the Left rule.

title: "No Payment Hurdle For Iranian Crude Imports": India Dismisses Rumours
content: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas dismissed reports that an oil tanker carrying Iranian crude had rerouted mid-voyage from its previously indicated destination of India to China.

title: Blindfolded, Made To Kneel: What Happened Last Time Iran Held US Personnel
content: Iran shot down a US F-15E fighter jet, marking the first confirmed loss of an American aircraft inside Iranian territory since the war began.

title: As Alliances Shift, 2 Bodoland Leaders Hope To Play 'Kingmaker' In Assam
content: In the Bodoland Territorial Region, the battle is primarily between Hagrama Mohilary and Pramod Boro.

title: Magnitude 6.0 Earthquake Strikes Off North Indonesia
content: A 6.0 magnitude quake struck off Indonesia's remote northern Talaud islands on Saturday, the US Geological Survey (USGS) said, but there were no immediate reports of casualties or damage.

title: Telangana Man Drowns Twin Daughters In Well. He Wanted A Son
content: A father allegedly killed his four-year-old twin daughters following frequent family disputes in Telangana's Karimnagar on Friday, officials said.

title: Amid War With Iran, UAE Asks Pakistan To Repay $2 Billion Loan: Report
content: The UAE recently asked for the immediate return of the funds in the wake of the recent situation in the Middle East following the US-Israel war on Iran.

title: IPL Match Theft Racket Busted In Bengaluru, 75 Phones Recovered
content: In a major breakthrough, police in Bengaluru have busted an organised interstate mobile theft racket that allegedly used trained minors to target spectators during an IPL 2026 match at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium.

title: May Affect Minority Institutions' Religious Freedom: Catholic Body On Foreign Funding Bill
content: Calling the move a "direct intrusion" into constitutionally protected spaces, the All India Catholic Union argued that the proposed amendments may conflict with Articles 25 and 26, which guarantee the right to freely practise religion and manage reli

title: 8 Indian Vessels Have Crossed Strait Of Hormuz, Among Highest In The World
content: Despite the blockade, India has emerged as one of the countries with the highest number of ships passing through this route. At least eight Indian vessels have passed through the Hormuz Strait since the conflict started.

title: Assam Minister Targets Congress, A Owaisi Over Zubin Garg, Uniform Code
content: Pijush Hazarika said the BJP-led government has undertaken several developmental works in the state, expressing confidence that the people of Assam will once again support the BJP and its alliance partners.

title: "Three Allegations. Zero Truth": Raghav Chadha vs AAP Escalates
content: Raghav Chahda, AAP's Punjab MP, was dropped as the party's Deputy Leader in the Rajya Sabha earlier this week. The party also said the Punjab MP should not be allotted time for speaking in the House from the AAP's quota.

title: Pakistan To Repay $3.5 Billion To UAE This Month Amid Strained Ties: Report
content: New Delhi, April 4 (IANS) Pakistan’s already weak economy is coming under fresh pressure as the country prepares to repay $3.5 billion to the United Arab Emirates this month, even as relations between the two sides show signs of strain.

title: Gujarat Man Kicked On Chest, Strangled To Death For Refusing To Lend Rs 10
content: According to the FIR, Dharmendra demanded Rs 10 for personal expenses, and when Rajeshbhai refused to give the money, the encounter turned deadly.

title: 'Let her play': Chiefs launch petition for high school girls' flag football in Kansas

title: Betting buzz: Men's Final Four features tightest spreads on record
content: our betting buzz file aims to provide fans a look at the sports betting stories that are driving the conversation . the men's final four will feature two of the tightest spreads in its history when it gets underway . it would be the first time since the tournament expanded to 64 teams in 1985 that both national semifinals had spreads of two or less .

title: Tracking fifth-year options for 2023 first-rounders: Who will return in 2027?
content: the deadline for teams to pick up fifth-year options on the rookie contracts of 2023 first-round draft picks is May 1 . if the team declines the option, the player remains under contract for 2027 . we are tracking the entire 2023 class, which had only 31 picks after the Miami Dolphins were stripped of their first round selection .
